comments.text: Gerver and Ramsey proved that there exists an infinite $S$-walk in $\mathbb{Z}^3$ that has no $5^{11}+1$ collinear points. Recent work by Lidbetter in Improved bound for the Gerver-Ramsey collinearity problem improved this bound to $189$. It is not known if there exists an $n$ such that there exists an infinite $S$-walk in $\mathbb{Z}^n$ that avoids $3$ collinear points.
